message.text: DynamoDB table source: point-in-time recovery (PITR) is disabled. Accidental writes/deletes are not recoverable to within-minute precision.
DynamoDB table source: server-side encryption is not explicitly enabled with a customer-managed KMS key. AWS-owned default encryption is always on, but lacks customer audit control.
DynamoDB table source: deletion_protection_enabled is false. An accidental terraform destroy or API call will permanently delete the table.
CloudTrail trail: is_multi_region_trail is false. API activity in other regions is unlogged — any attacker who pivots regions becomes invisible.
CloudTrail trail: log file integrity validation is disabled. Tampered log files cannot be detected.
CloudTrail trail: no CloudWatch Logs group is attached. Near-real-time alerting on suspicious API activity is not possible.
EventBridge bus existing_bus: no kms_key_identifier. Event payloads in transit through the bus rely on the AWS-managed default key — no CMK rotation, no per-customer audit boundary.
EventBridge bus external: no kms_key_identifier. Event payloads in transit through the bus rely on the AWS-managed default key — no CMK rotation, no per-customer audit boundary.
EventBridge bus target: no kms_key_identifier. Event payloads in transit through the bus rely on the AWS-managed default key — no CMK rotation, no per-customer audit boundary.

SQS queue queue: no redrive_policy (no dead-letter queue) and no kms_master_key_id. Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)

SQS queue fifo: no redrive_policy (no dead-letter queue) and no kms_master_key_id. Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)

Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)

Messages that fail repeatedly are dropped after the retention window, and at-rest data uses the default SQS-managed key (SSE-SQS) rather than a customer-managed CMK you control and audit. (Already a DLQ? Then the missing redrive_policy is expected.)
SQS queue queue: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue fifo: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue queue: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue products: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue source: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.
SQS queue target: no redrive_policy declared. Messages that fail to process retry until message_retention_seconds expires, then drop silently.

Firehose stream logs: S3 destination has no encryption_configuration. Records are buffered + delivered using the bucket's default encryption (often AES256). For CMK-required environments this fails the audit boundary.
Firehose stream logs: no cloudwatch_logging_options on the destination. Delivery failures (S3 PutObject errors, conversion errors, IAM denials) aren't surfaced to logs.

SQS queue queue: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

SQS queue fifo: no alarms reference this queue. Consumer death is invisible from the queue side until messages back up. ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage exceeding a few minutes is unambiguous evidence the consumer stopped processing.

Log group this: retention_in_days unset. Default is `Never Expire`. Logs accumulate at storage rates indefinitely; the only way out is a runbook to manually purge.
Log group target: retention_in_days unset. Default is `Never Expire`. Logs accumulate at storage rates indefinitely; the only way out is a runbook to manually purge.
Log group logs: retention_in_days unset. Default is `Never Expire`. Logs accumulate at storage rates indefinitely; the only way out is a runbook to manually purge.
IAM role with inline policy (should be managed policy): cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
CloudWatch Log Group this: no retention_in_days set. Logs are kept forever — at $0.03/GB/month, a moderately-active service generating 1 GB/day hits ~$900/month in log storage alone after 12 months.

CloudWatch Log Group target: no retention_in_days set. Logs are kept forever — at $0.03/GB/month, a moderately-active service generating 1 GB/day hits ~$900/month in log storage alone after 12 months.
CloudWatch Log Group logs: no retention_in_days set. Logs are kept forever — at $0.03/GB/month, a moderately-active service generating 1 GB/day hits ~$900/month in log storage alone after 12 months.
source: no lifecycle.prevent_destroy block. A terraform destroy or refactor that removes this resource address will delete it without a guardrail.
eventbridge: uses `count = condition ? 1 : 0` pattern. Changes to the condition logic can renumber the resource's address, causing terraform to plan a delete+create instead of an update.

Kinesis stream this: records are not encrypted at rest (encryption_type = NONE or absent). Stream data including PII, financial events, and clickstream records is stored in plaintext on AWS-managed infrastructure.

Kinesis stream source: records are not encrypted at rest (encryption_type = NONE or absent). Stream data including PII, financial events, and clickstream records is stored in plaintext on AWS-managed infrastructure.

Kinesis stream target: records are not encrypted at rest (encryption_type = NONE or absent). Stream data including PII, financial events, and clickstream records is stored in plaintext on AWS-managed infrastructure.

Kinesis stream this: records are not encrypted at rest (encryption_type = NONE or absent). Stream data including PII, financial events, and clickstream records is stored in plaintext on AWS-managed infrastructure.

CloudWatch log group this: no retention_in_days is set. Logs accumulate indefinitely — CloudWatch Logs storage is $0.03/GB/month with no automatic pruning. A single high-volume application (e.g., ECS service, Lambda) can silently accumulate hundreds of GB over months.

CloudWatch log group target: no retention_in_days is set. Logs accumulate indefinitely — CloudWatch Logs storage is $0.03/GB/month with no automatic pruning. A single high-volume application (e.g., ECS service, Lambda) can silently accumulate hundreds of GB over months.

CloudWatch log group logs: no retention_in_days is set. Logs accumulate indefinitely — CloudWatch Logs storage is $0.03/GB/month with no automatic pruning. A single high-volume application (e.g., ECS service, Lambda) can silently accumulate hundreds of GB over months.
AWS provider: no default_tags block. Every resource has to tag itself for Cost Explorer — teams forget on a third of them.

Log groups (this, target, logs) have no retention_in_days set. CloudWatch keeps logs forever by default at $0.03/GB-month. A high-traffic service generating 10 GB/day accumulates ~$110/month in storage after one year with no bound in sight.
EventBridge rule this: no aws_lambda_permission references this rule's arn. If a target is a Lambda, invocations silently 403 — events are dropped without diagnostics.
IAM role eventbridge: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.
IAM role eventbridge_pipe: permissions_boundary unset. Future policy attachments can grant unlimited privilege — the role's effective permissions = union of all attached policies, no ceiling.
IAM role has no permissions_boundary: cannot verify — required attribute(s) missing from source: name
